note: Cofactor facts extracted from the bundled textbooks. Useful for the agent's get_nutrition_facts + search_knowledge chain in the Streamlit demo.

facts.description: Vitamin C consumed in the same meal enhances non-heme iron absorption by forming a soluble complex that resists phytate/polyphenol inhibition.
Fat-soluble vitamins A/D/E/K are absorbed together with dietary fat; include a small amount of oil/nuts with orange vegetables.
Vitamin D absorption improves when consumed with dietary fat (egg yolks, fortified milk with a small amount of fat, fatty fish).
Vitamin E is fat-soluble; paired with nuts, seeds, or oils for optimal absorption.
Vitamin K in leafy greens is better absorbed with a small amount of fat (olive oil dressing).
Phytic acid in whole grains, legumes, and nuts can reduce non-heme iron absorption; pair with vitamin C to mitigate.
Polyphenols in tea and coffee inhibit non-heme iron absorption when consumed with a meal.
High-dose calcium consumed alongside iron can reduce iron absorption; space supplemental calcium away from iron-rich meals.
Vitamin D is required for efficient calcium absorption in the small intestine.
Magnesium is a cofactor for enzymes that activate vitamin D in the body.
